heading: Transition to Harmonized Tariff Schedule
label: 3011 Transition to Harmonized Tariff Schedule
notes: References in TextThe Harmonized Tariff Schedule, referred to in subsecs. (a)(1), (c), and (d)(2)(B), (3), is not set out in the Code. See Publication of Harmonized Tariff Schedule note set out under .section 1202 of this titleThis chapter, referred to in subsec. (d)(1), was in the original “this subtitle”, meaning subtitle B (§§ 1201–1217) of title I of , which is classified principally to this chapter. For complete classification of this subtitle to the Code, see References in Text note set out under and Tables.Pub. L. 100–418section 3001 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: are in effect on the day before ; andJanuary 1, 1989

children.label: B
children.text: contain references to the tariff classification of articles under the old Schedules.

Neither the repeal of the old Schedules, nor the failure of any officer of the United States Government to make the conforming changes required under paragraph (1), shall affect to any extent the validity or effect of the proclamation, regulation, ruling, notice, finding, determination, order, recommendation, or other action referred to in paragraph (1).

children.heading: Generalized System of Preferences conversion
children.label: b Generalized System of Preferences conversion

children.text: The review of the proposed conversion of the Generalized System of Preferences program to the Convention tariff nomenclature, initiated by the Office of the United States Trade Representative by notice published in the Federal Register on (at page 44,163 of volume 51 thereof), shall be treated as satisfying the requirements of sections 2463(a) and 2464(c)(3) of this title (as in effect on ).December 8, 1986July 31, 1995
In applying (as in effect on ) for calendar year 1989, the reference in such section to July 1 shall be treated as a reference to September 1.section 2464(c)(1) of this titleJuly 31, 1995

children.heading: Import restrictions under Agricultural Adjustment Act
children.label: c Import restrictions under Agricultural Adjustment Act
A
children.text: an article that was previously subject to the restriction being excluded from the restriction; or

children.label: B
children.text: an article not previously subject to the restriction being included within the restriction;

children.label: A
children.text: an article that was previously covered by such headnote being excluded from coverage; or

children.label: B
children.text: an article not previously covered by such headnote being included in coverage;
No change to the Harmonized Tariff Schedule may be proclaimed under paragraph (1) or (2) after .June 30, 1990

children.heading: Certain protests and petitions under customs law
children.label: d Certain protests and petitions under customs law
i
children.text: any protest filed under ; orsection 1514 of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: any petition by an American manufacturer, producer, or wholesaler under ;section 1516 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: Nothing in this chapter shall affect the jurisdiction of the courts with respect to articles entered after .January 1, 1989

children.label: A
children.text: If any protest or petition referred to in paragraph (1)(A) is sustained in whole or in part by a final judicial decision, the entries subject to that protest or petition and made before , shall be liquidated or reliquidated, as appropriate, in accordance with such final judicial decision under the old Schedules.January 1, 1989

children.label: ii
children.text: would have affected tariff treatment if they had been published during the period of the conversion of the old Schedules into the format of the Convention.

children.label: A
children.text: entries made on or after the date of such proclamation; and

children.label: B
children.text: entries made on or after , if, notwithstanding , application for liquidation or reliquidation thereof is made by the importer to the customs officer concerned within 180 days after the effective date of such proclamation.January 1, 1989section 1514 of this title

If any protest or petition referred to in paragraph (1)(A) is not sustained in whole or in part by a final judicial decision, the entries subject to that petition or protest and made before , shall be liquidated or reliquidated, as appropriate, in accordance with the final judicial decision under the old Schedules.January 1, 1989
